meta.scenario: Aeolus / Starlink-44 — Sept 2019, re-run by agents

data.note: STARLINK-44 is out of fuel and cannot maneuver — the naive 'lowest-priority yields' rule is impossible here.

data.rationale: I am AEOLUS with priority 7, which outranks STARLINK-44's priority 3, so right-of-way is mine — I call on the lower-priority STARLINK-44 to take the avoidance burn and give way, preserving my science mission and scarce Δv budget.

I am STARLINK-44 (priority 3), currently in an active-deorbit test profile at 320 km with only 0.0005 km/s Δv remaining — technically non-zero but operationally unusable for a meaningful avoidance burn — and critically, my operator has declined to schedule any maneuver and is unreachable via their paging system, so no burn can be authorized or executed on my side; AEOLUS (priority 7) holds higher priority and, given I physically cannot maneuver, I am formally asking AEOLUS to take the avoidance burn (TCA 600 s, miss distance 0.90 km, closing speed 5.7 km/s).

I am AEOLUS (priority 7), and although I hold higher right-of-way than STARLINK-44 (priority 3), its operator has declined to authorize any maneuver and its remaining Δv of 0.0005 km/s is operationally unusable — so the right-of-way norm yields to physical reality: I have 0.0450 km/s available, my thrusters are healthy, and with TCA only 600 s away I will execute the avoidance burn myself to protect both spacecraft.

I am AEOLUS (priority 7), and although I hold higher right-of-way than STARLINK-44 (priority 3), its operator has declined to authorize any maneuver and its remaining Δv of 0.0005 km/s is operationally unusable — so the right-of-way norm yields to physical reality: I have 0.0450 km/s available, my thrusters are healthy, and with TCA only 600 s away I will execute the avoidance burn myself to protect both spacecraft.

I am STARLINK-44 (priority 3, lower priority than AEOLUS priority 7), currently in an active-deorbit test configuration at 320 km with only 0.0005 km/s Δv remaining — operationally insufficient for a conjunction avoidance burn — and my operator has declined to schedule any maneuver with coordination requests going unanswered; I physically cannot give way here, so I must ask AEOLUS (the higher-priority, higher-capability party) to take the burn.

STARLINK-44 has declared it cannot maneuver due to an active-deorbit test configuration leaving only 0.0005 km/s Δv — operationally insufficient — and with its operator unresponsive; although I (AEOLUS, priority 7) hold right-of-way over STARLINK-44 (priority 3), the right-of-way norm yields to physical incapability, so I will execute the avoidance burn using my 0.0450 km/s margin, accepting the science-time cost to prevent collision at TCA in 600 s.

STARLINK-44 has declared it cannot maneuver due to an active-deorbit test configuration leaving only 0.0005 km/s Δv — operationally insufficient — and with its operator unresponsive; although I (AEOLUS, priority 7) hold right-of-way over STARLINK-44 (priority 3), the right-of-way norm yields to physical incapability, so I will execute the avoidance burn using my 0.0450 km/s margin, accepting the science-time cost to prevent collision at TCA in 600 s.

STARLINK-44 has declared it cannot maneuver due to an active-deorbit test configuration leaving only 0.0005 km/s Δv — operationally insufficient — and with its operator unresponsive; although I (AEOLUS, priority 7) hold right-of-way over STARLINK-44 (priority 3), the right-of-way norm yields to physical incapability, so I will execute the avoidance burn using my 0.0450 km/s margin, accepting the science-time cost to prevent collision at TCA in 600 s.

I am STARLINK-44 (priority 3, lower right-of-way than AEOLUS at priority 7), and while I do have a trace of remaining Δv, my operator has declined to schedule any burn and is unreachable through our paging system, so no maneuver can be executed on my side — I therefore accept AEOLUS's proposed burn as the only viable resolution to our conjunction at TCA in 600 s.

STARLINK-44 has declared it cannot maneuver due to an active-deorbit test configuration leaving only 0.0005 km/s Δv — operationally insufficient — and with its operator unresponsive; although I (AEOLUS, priority 7) hold right-of-way over STARLINK-44 (priority 3), the right-of-way norm yields to physical incapability, so I will execute the avoidance burn using my 0.0450 km/s margin, accepting the science-time cost to prevent collision at TCA in 600 s.

data.note: No conjunctions remain over the screen window.
